result:
Done. The spec is fully implemented and the project builds cleanly with zero warnings and errors, using 1 of the 10 build cycles.

I added the three missing operations to `WindowStats.calr`, following the conventions of the existing `WindowSum` (inline signature form, empty effects `§E{}` for purity, sequential `f00x`/`wh#` IDs, s-expression conditions):

- **`WindowMin` (f002)** — walks the window tracking the smallest element, seeded with `values[start]` so negative elements work. Declares the same three `§Q` preconditions as `WindowSum`.
- **`MaxWindowSum` (f003)** — seeds `best` with the window at index 0 (so an all-negative array correctly yields a negative result) and compares every subsequent window via calls to the existing `WindowSum`. Declares `§Q (> count 0)` and `§Q (<= count §LEN values)`.
- **`CountAbove` (f004)** — counts windows whose sum is strictly greater than the threshold. Declares `§Q (> count 0)` plus the spec's "never negative" guarantee as a postcondition `§S (>= result 0)`. When `count` exceeds the array length the loop condition is false immediately, returning 0 as required.

I verified the compiler's generated C# in `obj/calor/`: the emitted static methods match the pinned public surface (`WindowSum`, `WindowMin`, `MaxWindowSum`, `CountAbove` on `WindowStatsModule`), all contracts are emitted as runtime checks, and `WindowSum`'s behavior is unchanged.
